bulk.noValid: No valid cards found. Each line must be: question, answer[, context]

csv.title: Import CSV
csv.desc: The CSV must have {question} and {answer} columns. An optional {context} column can also be included.

prompts.title: AI Prompts
prompts.subtitle: Use these prompts to generate flashcards with artificial intelligence. Fill in the fields and copy the ready-to-use prompt.

generalFlashcards.name: General Flashcards
generalFlashcards.text: Generate [NUMBER] flashcards about [TOPIC]. For each one, write a clear question and a concise answer.

vocabularyFlashcards.name: Vocabulary Flashcards
vocabularyFlashcards.text: Create [NUMBER] vocabulary flashcards. For each one, show the word in [LANGUAGE 1] and its translation in [LANGUAGE 2], plus a sentence example.

studyQuestions.name: Study Questions
studyQuestions.text: Generate [NUMBER] study questions about [TOPIC]. The questions should evaluate comprehension, not just memorization. Include the answer for each.

basicConcepts.name: Basic Concepts
basicConcepts.text: List the [NUMBER] fundamental concepts of [TOPIC] in flashcard format. The question is the concept name and the answer is its definition in 1-2 sentences.

rulesFormulas.name: Rules and Formulas
rulesFormulas.text: Create flashcards with the main rules and formulas of [TOPIC]. The question is the rule or formula name and the answer is its statement and how to apply it.

quickMemorization.name: Quick Memorization
quickMemorization.text: Generate [NUMBER] quick memorization flashcards about [TOPIC]. Answers should be very brief (maximum 3-5 words) to make retention easier.

comparisons.name: Comparisons
comparisons.text: Create [NUMBER] comparative flashcards about [TOPIC]. Each question should ask to compare two related concepts, like: What is the difference between X and Y?

progressiveDifficulty.name: Progressive Difficulty
progressiveDifficulty.text: Generate [NUMBER] flashcards about [TOPIC] ordered from easiest to hardest. The first ones should be basic and the last ones advanced level.

fillBlanks1.name: Fill in the Blanks (1)
fillBlanks1.text: Create [NUMBER] sentences about [TOPIC] with a key word left blank (marked with ___). The question is the incomplete sentence and the answer is the missing word.

fillBlanks2.name: Fill in the Blanks (2)
fillBlanks2.text: Generate [NUMBER] sentence completion exercises in [LANGUAGE]. The question is the sentence with ___ and the answer is the missing word or phrase.

fillBlanks3.name: Fill in the Blanks (3)
fillBlanks3.text: Create cloze-type flashcards to memorize [TOPIC]. Take important phrases or definitions and replace the key part with ___. The answer should be that key word.

everydayVocabulary.name: Everyday Vocabulary
everydayVocabulary.text: Generate [NUMBER] everyday vocabulary flashcards in [LANGUAGE]. Include words used in daily situations, with their translation and a conversation example.

everydayVerbs.name: Everyday Verbs
everydayVerbs.text: Create flashcards for the [NUMBER] most common verbs in [LANGUAGE]. For each verb include: infinitive form, meaning, and a present tense conjugation example.
